calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 No Appearance; Order To Arrest

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Continued For Letter To Feb-04-26 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for an Order of Examination as to Judgment Debtor Wheelcare Express, Inc.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505; Matter not reported

Judgment Creditor Wesley Warner is present represented by Rush James.
Judgment Debtor Wheelcare Express, Inc. is not present.

The court finds there is valid proof of service on file.

As there is no appearance by the Judgment Debtor, a warrant is issued and stayed until the next calling.

A Bench Letter is generated and mailed to Chanchira Nariunanout named the person most knowledgable.

This matter is continued to February 4, 2026 at 11:00 in dept. 505. If there is no appearance at the next calling, Mr. James may request a warrant.

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Aug-22-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2024 Off Calendar

Request For Entry Of Court Default Judgment At Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant Wheelcare Express, Inc.

Dept.505: Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 9:31:16 a.m., pausing at 10:19:57 a.m., resuming at 10:30:30 a.m., pausing at 10:33:22 a.m., resuming at 10:34:43 a.m. and concluding at 10:45:53 a.m. (D505)

James Rush, Esq. representing Plaintiff Wesley Warner via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Wesley Warner was placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Wheelcare Express, Inc. not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint for personal injury filed Nov-09-2023.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant Wheelcare Express, Inc. mailed on Dec-01-2023; effectuated on Dec-11-2023.
Default entered as to Defendant Wheelcare Express, Inc. on Feb-14-2024.
Proof of service of Default packet filed Jun-11-2024.

Counsel to summarize the facts of the case.
Counsel examines Plaintiff.
Plaintiff testifies in detail as to the incident and cause of the action.
Counsel concludes the examination.
The Court examines the Plaintiff.
Plaintiff testifies at length as to the incident and injuries sustained.
Court inquires with Counsel as to how to proceed in absence of the medical records exhibit.
The Court reviews the amount prayed for.
The Court takes judicial notice of the following exhibits attached to Counsel's declaration:
Exhibit B: Summons and Complaint
Exhibit C: Secretary of State, statement of information
Exhibit D: Non Service Report
Exhibit E: Defendant's statements of information
Exhibit F: Proof of service
Exhibit G: Statement of Damages
Exhibit H: Default against defendant
Exhibit I: Order of continuing case management
Counsel to lay foundation for Exhibit A: Incident report and Exhibit J: Plaintiff's medical bills.

The Court takes a brief recess for counsel to confer with client.
Proceedings are back on the record.
Counsel for plaintiff is present. Plaintiff is having issues logging back into Zoom video Conference.
Court goes off the record.
Proceedings are back on the record.
Counsel and plaintiff are present via Zoom Video conference.

Counsel examines Plaintiff as to Exhibit A.
Court examines Plaintiff as to Exhibit A.
Court examines Plaintiff as to Exhibit J.
The Court finds that Exbibit J lacks foundation.

The Court finds that the evidence comports to proof that the Defendant was negligent and rules in favor of the Plaintiff.

The Plaintiff is to recover from the Defendant as follows:
Damages $150,000.00
Interest $0
Attorney's Fees $0
Costs $1,236.00
Total Judgment $151,236.00

Proposed judgment as amended signed and executed this day.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-15-2024 Continued To Aug-14-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
